quote_of_day.text: I failed 12th grade and some 10/10 girl rejected me because i am kallu and short. I will blame this on casteism because chamarin didi on instagram, pasmanda bhaiya on xitter and bhangi bhaiya on youtube says that all your problems exist because of casteism.
quote_of_day.name: Bablu kumar from Begusarai

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of moderator frustration moved into the board this morning, with Anon calling for intervention in a heated thread. This sparked a downpour of replies. pouring in.

A scattered drizzle of fitness advice fell across the board in the afternoon, as Anons discussed ways to hit protein goals on a budget.

This thread drew a steady stream of respondents. accumulating. Meanwhile, a cold front of dissatisfaction with the current board configuration blew in, bringing with it a chill of discontent.

Anons expressed their desire for new chan configurations. registering their discontent.

articles.title: Anon Sparks Controversy, Calls for Moderator Intervention
articles.body:
Anon kicked off a heated discussion by simply posting 'MODS!!' on the board.

One anon responded by referencing a previous thread, stating it was an 'organic movement' that should have been allowed to continue, suggesting it could have led to entertaining content.

Another anon advised moderators to minimize their interference, warning that excessive presence could make the board resemble another, less popular platform. This anon suggested browsing the site like a regular anon and only using admin mode for necessary deletions.

A different anon chimed in, cautioning moderators about the 'butterfly effect' of their actions, urging restraint when considering deletions. The conversation took a personal turn when an anon accused a specific individual of being overly sensitive and making cringeworthy comments.

One anon joked about the potential for epilepsy-inducing content and speculated about the prevalence of epilepsy among users due to genetic factors.

An anon claimed to have evidence of a previous post from the same user on another platform and offered to find archived images. Another anon claimed to have screencapped an entire thread but currently lacks access to their device.

The discussion also touched on the topic of moderators' power and responsibility, with one anon noting they could delete threads but risked allegations of bias.

The thread included various personal attacks and insults, with one anon boasting about having 'raped' another user in a previous thread.

Throughout the conversation, users debated the role of moderators, the impact of their actions, and the overall dynamics of the board.

articles.title: Hitting protein goals on a budget
articles.body:
Anon was struggling to get enough protein on a monthly stipend of 1500 rupees, aiming for at least 60g of protein per day.

They found it difficult with eggs providing only 6g each and meat cooked in oil being too calorie-dense.

One respondent suggested looking into the bioavailability and DIAAS scores of various foods, noting that the body absorbs protein from eggs and chicken at nearly 100%, while plant-based proteins like chana and lentils are absorbed at around 50-60%.

However, another anon disputed this claim, stating that the bioavailability of all protein is the same and that the perceived lower quality of veg protein is due to assumptions about digestion.

A different approach was suggested by an anon who calculated that buying eggs could be a cost-effective way to get protein, with an egg tray of 30 eggs costing 220 rupees and providing 37.8g of protein per day.

Some anons shared their own experiences with obtaining chicken, with one asking how to ensure good quality chicken. Another anon recommended slowly ramping up protein intake to avoid digestive issues.

The discussion also devolved into personal attacks, with anons insulting each other's intelligence and backgrounds.

articles.title: Anons Discuss New Chan and Board Configurations
articles.body:
Anon expressed dissatisfaction with the current state of Indiachan, calling it a "kanglu infested shithole" and looking forward to new chans being developed.

Another anon responded, mentioning that they had considered creating multiple boards to increase activity, but noted that previous attempts by "dyaush" to create separate boards had failed, with them eventually becoming inactive.

One respondent criticized the complexity of software for digital art, stating that the need to spend a month learning a program before starting to draw was a significant deterrent.

A reply suggested that one did not need to learn everything, and that simple approaches, such as using a 32x32 grid, could be effective.

The topic of board configurations was also discussed, with one anon suggesting a "/fit/" board and another proposing a "/lit/" board for literature and religious discussions.

Anon also joked about the target audience of new boards, stating that they were being created for the same 15 anons.

There was also a brief discussion about the migration to new software, with one anon announcing that they had purchased a VPS and would have Induschan up by the next day.

articles.title: Anon Cravings 2D Waifus
articles.body:
Anon opened a thread asking for help getting 2D waifus and joking about being 'gobard'. The original poster expressed frustration, saying "MUJHE MERI 2D WAIFUS DILA DO".

Anon responded with fantasies about having a robot waifu, listing benefits like no STDs and no emotional drama. Another anon joked about hiring a cosplayer and imagining them as their waifu.

Anon also discussed cosplay and ethnic differences, with one saying jeetas look ugly in cosplay while others look cute. Anon praised a Bihari cosplayer and discussed YouTube and Instagram.

articles.title: Anon mocks pasmanda's gif upload fail
articles.body:
Anon called out 'pasmanda' for allegedly spamming low-IQ content and inability to upload a gif.
The poster claimed pasmanda only repeats 'samefagging' and content from plebbit. Anon shared a way to upload gifs, sarcastically remarking 'NEET Summer break chal rahe abhi'.
Replies included insults towards pasmanda and another user named 'Haggu'. The tone was mocking and aggressive.
